hypothesis: If NN-BAX iteratively fine-tunes EquiformerV2 and runs NEB on the learned model with acquisition sampling, then it will reproduce ground-truth NEB minimum-energy paths for LJ7 and LJ38 transitions (meeting the same fmax and MAEi convergence criteria) while requiring significantly fewer expensive simulator force evaluations than classical NEB, because the model learns the local potential-energy surface along the path and guides optimization efficiently.
